context: Stephanie Wells sole-proprietor mentoring side business; rules for what counts as a deductible business expense versus personal spend, kept separate from wedding budget.

validation_rules.notes: Zoom, Notion, Calendly, Canva, Google Workspace used for mentoring
validation_rules.requirement: Tool is used for client sessions or scheduling
validation_rules.rule: Recurring SaaS tools are deductible business expenses

validation_rules.notes: PM books, courses, and conference fees
validation_rules.requirement: Directly improves mentoring or PM craft
validation_rules.rule: Learning and reference materials are deductible

validation_rules.notes: Coffee or meals with a mentee, capped at 50 percent deductible
validation_rules.requirement: Receipt names attendees and business purpose

validation_rules.notes: Wedding deposits and personal purchases tracked in the separate wedding budget
validation_rules.requirement: Expense is not tied to mentoring revenue
validation_rules.rule: Personal and wedding costs are not business deductions

validation_rules.notes: Stripe and bank fees on mentoring invoices
validation_rules.requirement: Fee is charged on a business payout
